goals.goal: One log envelope shared by backend and frontend. Byte-identical field names and types.
Every user-visible operation correlatable end-to-end via a single id.
No PII in logs. Privacy is enforced by code, not discipline.
Business events are a closed registry. Any new event is a canon patch.
Logs are machine-parsable JSON in prod, human-readable in dev.

non-goals.non-goal: Distributed tracing (OpenTelemetry) — out of scope until PHASE-6+.
User-facing audit log — separate domain, separate table.

field.name: correlation_id
field.desc: One id per user-visible operation. Propagated via X-Correlation-Id header. Frontend mints on user action, backend reuses or mints if absent.

field.name: session_id
field.desc: Per-tab session, frontend only; backend mirrors when received.

field.name: slice
field.desc: GRACE wave/slice id (e.g. 'W-1.2', 'W-HORARY'). Identifies the initiative that owns the code path.

field.name: module
field.desc: Module contract id (e.g. 'M-AUTH-TG.api', 'M-PROFILE.service'). Maps one-to-one with START_MODULE_CONTRACT names.

field.name: block
field.desc: Semantic block id from GRACE_ANCHORS or START_BLOCK comment (e.g. 'ROUTE_AUTH_TG', 'PROFILE_UPDATE'). Identifies the specific code block emitting the event.

field.name: phase
field.desc: Phase-level orchestration id for multi-phase operations (e.g. 'phase-1', 'credit-verify').

field.name: operation_id
field.desc: Id for multi-step background jobs. Generated per job instance.

field.name: event
field.desc: Either business-event name from §8.5 registry, or technical event 'system.<verb>' (system.request, system.error, system.startup, system.shutdown). Validated against codegen-generated enum.

field.name: msg
field.desc: Human-readable single-line message. No PII. Max 500 chars; truncate with ellipsis.

field.name: payload
field.desc: Event-specific structured fields. Schema per event in §8.5. Passed through redactor.

field.name: error
field.desc: Present iff level in {error,fatal}. Shape: {kind, message, stack?}. message and stack pass redactor.

field.name: http
field.desc: Shape: {method, route, status, ip_hash?}. route is the templated path '/api/day/:date', NEVER the raw URL.

envelope.rule: Unknown fields are dropped at emit. Schema is closed at the envelope level.
Envelope is identical on back and front. Codegen produces TS type and Python TypedDict from this XML.

level.name: debug
level.desc: Developer-only. Stripped in prod by default; opt-in via GRACE_LOG_DEBUG=1 per service.

level.name: error
level.desc: Operation failed but service continues. Always logged.

rule.desc: Phase 1: log all requests. Sampling deferred until volume justifies it; ratio change is a canon patch.

privacy.principle: Default-deny: any key not on the allow-list is treated as opaque payload and passes through redactor key-matching.
Redaction runs on both back and front, before any transport (console, file, ship).
Replacement value is the literal string "[redacted]" (or category-specific marker for regex hits).

correlation-substitute.rule: Where raw user identity is needed for joining logs, emit `user_id_hash = sha256(tg_user_id || GRACE_USER_SALT)[0:12]`.
GRACE_USER_SALT is a server-only secret. Hash MUST NOT be reversible without it.
Frontend computes user_id_hash only after backend hands it back via /api/auth/telegram response. Frontend never sees raw tg_user_id again after that handshake.

events.rule: Adding, removing, or renaming an event is a canon patch (W-CANON-LOG-EXT-N microwave).

Codegen produces: - apps/api/app/core/logging_events.py (Python `Literal[...]` + payload TypedDicts) - lib/log/events.gen.ts (TS string-literal union + payload interfaces) Both are committed; CI fails on drift between this XML and generated artifacts.
`log.event(name, payload)` is the only emitter for business events. It is typed; opcodes outside the registry fail to compile.

Technical events use the `system.*` namespace and are emitted by infra helpers, not application code. They are listed below alongside business events for completeness, but are not part of the user-facing taxonomy.

event.name: system.request
event.desc: HTTP request completed (server side) or fetch completed (client side).

event.name: system.error
event.desc: Unhandled exception caught by global handler.

event.name: system.config_loaded
event.desc: Configuration loaded at startup; emits a redacted summary of effective settings.

event.name: profile.updated
event.desc: Birth data or display fields changed. Payload MUST NOT contain the new values; only field names that changed.

correlation.rule: Frontend mints `correlation_id = uuidv4()` at the start of every user-initiated operation (button click, route change, form submit).
All `fetch` / API calls in that operation carry it as `X-Correlation-Id` header.
Backend middleware reads `X-Correlation-Id`; if missing or malformed, mints a new one. Echoes it back as response header `X-Correlation-Id`.
Backend stores it in a context-var (Python contextvars / asyncio task-local) for the request scope. All log emissions in that scope auto-attach it.
Frontend resolves the response header and includes the resolved id in the next log line for that operation, ensuring back-front join.
Sidecar (apps/solarsage) accepts and propagates the same header.

transport.name: stdout-json
transport.desc: Single-line JSON per envelope. One log per line, UTF-8, LF-terminated.

transport.name: stderr-pretty
transport.desc: Human-readable: `[ts] LEVEL service event corr=… msg`. Color OK in TTY only.

transport.name: console
transport.desc: Browser devtools console, grouped by correlation_id.

transport.name: ship-http
transport.desc: POST batches to /api/_log. Lands in W-1.7, gated by GRACE_LOG_SHIPPING=on.

gate.check: Generated artifacts (logging_events.py, events.gen.ts) match this XML byte-for-byte.
Every `log.event(name, ...)` call site uses a name from the registry (TS/mypy compile-time + grep audit).
Every emitted event in CI test logs has a payload conforming to its declared payload-fields.
gate.on-fail: CI fails the PR. Fix is either a canon patch or a code change.

gate.check: For each redact-key in §8.4, a canary unit test emits a log line with that key set to a sentinel value and asserts the sentinel does not appear in the serialized output.
For each redact-pattern, a canary unit test emits a value matching the pattern and asserts the pattern's replacement marker is present.
Every log line in CI test output (api + web jest + e2e) parses as JSON and validates against the envelope schema generated from §8.2.

deferred.item: Long-term storage / log warehouse → infra concern, separate canon section.
